summary: Add svg import conversion tool

description.text: Closes [godotengine/godot-proposals#12770](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12770). This PR adds a new tool to the Project > Tools menu (currently titled: "Convert SVG Import Files from Texture2D to SVGTexture") . When run, it finds all of the svg files in the project and converts their importer to SVGTexture from Texture2D, causing the oversampling feature work for them. Here is an adapted project where i had imported the editor icons used in the main scene to Texture2D [adapted_test_project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21179260/adapted_test_project.zip) The code in this PR is adapted from the "Upgrade Project Files..." tool (ProjectUpgradeTool in the source code, and the pr format has been adapted from the [Add UID upgrade tool PR](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/102071) ). --- > Had initially thought of doing this for my project which had a lot of svg files that were still imported as CompressedTexture2Ds in 4.5-beta3 (makes sense, if using custom import settings that can't be used currently and do not want them being erased). I didn't have any such import settings so thought of making an tool that helps with the conversion...

summary: (Editor Visual Only?) C# EnumBytes incorrectly/inconsistently treated as Signed Byte when used in [Tool] Resources.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested on Godot Version 4.4.1 and 4.5 Beta 3.

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta3.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7602) - AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 63.62 GiB memory

### Issue description

Resources with the [Tool] flag incorrectly interpret C# Enum : byte as Signed Byte in inconsistent ways.

Ex: In menu displays, selecting a value above 128 correctly shows the enum name.  However selecting the value, instead displays the negative signed numeric value in the resource display, and the resulting .tscn will save a negative, signed value to the resource.

This seems to mostly(?) be a visual bug, as accessing the values via C# seems to return the correct enum value.  However, I don't know/can't test all possible situations where this might create issues.

### Steps to reproduce

See Minimum reproduction project attached.

>Select the Non-Tool Resource.
>Select values above 127 for the fields/properties of the resource.
>Observe the correct enum value display.

>Select the Tool Resource.
>Select values above 127 for the fields/properties of the resource.
>Observe the incorrect, signed negative byte value displayed, rather than the Enum value.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[ByteEnumResourceBugProject.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21171869/ByteEnumResourceBugProject.zip)

summary: GDScript LSP: BBCode conversion to markdown is missing various supported tags

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in Godot 4.4.1 and previous versions. The code implementing the bbcode to markdown conversion in the LSP has not changed over the past 6 years. ### System information Linux Pop!_OS 22.04 LTS 64-bit ### Issue description In the GDScript language server, the conversion of bbcode to markdown is incomplete and leads to docstrings not rendering well in external code editors. Screenshot from the MRP, with a docstring showing up in Zed: In VSCode docstrings appear fine, but that's because the VSCode extension cleans up the invalid markdown sent by the GDScript language server: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-vscode-plugin/blob/45db62bfa312b820548ef7d12be18e8caf4214c1/src/lsp/GDScriptLanguageClient.ts#L224-L247 Instead, this should be addressed in core because: 1. Every external editor plugin would need to implement the fix individually 2. There are still edge cases left and supported bbcode tags not handled, neither in the editor or the plugin 3. Actually, in some editors like Zed, I think right now it's not even possible to intercept and process language server RPC messages freely in extensions; language server features and fixes are expected to be made upstream in the language servers themselves HolonProduction was so kind to point me to the code converting the bbcode, which is located here: https://github.com/HolonProduction/godot/blob/53be3b78d1634406f1fb29e3802c608a5f5104a1/modules/gdscript/language_server/godot_lsp.h#L1941-L1991 I'll follow the issue with a draft pull request. ### Steps to reproduce - Open an external editor like Zed that connects to the GDScript language server - Hover over a project symbol with a docstring including bbcode formatting, including tags like [br], [codeblock lang=gdscript], [color=red], etc. (basic formatting like bold or italics works fine) ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [bbcode_docstrings_MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21171363/bbcode_docstrings_MRP.zip)

description.text:
### Tested versions

### System information

### Issue description

I'm building an editor plugin, and I am adding to the editor's scene tree with internal nodes to hide from the user.
Saving the .tscn file saves the internal nodes as regular nodes.

Checking the .tscn file in a txt editor, there is no parameter/token to differentiate an internal node from a regular node.

3. Save and reload the project.

summary: Make editor property clipboard static

description.text:
In the inspector, the Copy/Paste Value feature in a property's context menu is implemented via an internal property clipboard. This is because the value copied is a Variant instead of a simple string.

Currently each `EditorInspector` instance has a property clipboard. However, it is always the property clipboard of the Inspector dock's instance that is used by the copy/paste feature for all properties. This usage is necessary because we need to support pasting across different `EditorInspector` instances.

This PR makes the property clipboard a static member of `EditorInspector`.

summary: C# Exported Array won't reference nodes after cache clean.
Make file part of errors/warnings clickable in Output panel

description.text:
Fixes godotengine/godot-proposals#1628.
Fixes godotengine/godot-proposals#10000.
Relates to #57896.
Relates to #87216.

This makes the file and line part of error messages in the Output panel clickable, by simply wrapping both in a `[url]` BBCode tag, parsing those URIs in `EditorLog::_meta_clicked` and then opening the file in much the same way as the editor normally would, either in the built-in script editor or external editor, depending on the user's editor settings.

This PR takes a more conservative approach compared to #57896, by not trying to find every possible URL in the log and instead only concerning itself with the thing we already know is a file path, which saves us from any costly/error-prone parsing of the log itself.

One potentially controversial change here is that `EditorLog::_meta_clicked` will now early-out if it gets passed an invalid URI, meaning anything that doesn't start with something like `res:`, `file:`, `mailto:` or whatever else. The reason for this being that errors/warnings emitted from C++ will include relative file paths (e.g. `editor/editor_log.cpp:201`) which I couldn't see an easy way to deal with, and forwarding those to `OS::shell_open` will typically open the user's browser at that (bogus) path, so I opted to have it do nothing instead.

We could of course just not wrap the relative C++ paths in `[url]` to begin with, but again, I wanted to avoid any type of costly/error-prone parsing during logging if possible.

I'm happy to change it back to default to `OS::shell_open` for all non-URIs, but I figure since the express intent of #87216 was to allow for things like `file:` and `mailto:` then this behavior might be desirable anyway.

summary: Fix some Text Editor theme issues and clean up

description.text:
- related https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107918

Changes
- Fixed `Reload Theme`, it did not seem to work at all. It was probably broken since https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/69032, since `EditorSettings::load_text_editor_theme()` emitted the signal without marking the settings as changed, but it could be from earlier.
- Removed `Save Theme` menu option. It was not very useful because if you are on a loaded theme, then the file content is already the same. And if you change any theme setting in EditorSettings it switches to `Custom` theme which always directs you to the `Save Theme as...` option.
- `Import theme` now also immediately switches to the new theme. This is because if you don't know that it is copying a file to the themes directory, it gave no feedback that it worked.
- Moved the saving and importing to the ScriptEditor with the options, they don't need to be in the EditorSettings.
- When saving a new text editor theme it no longer sets the initial value for the `color_theme`, it removed the revert button for it unnecessarily and it didn't persist after restart anyway.
- Switched to toasters instead of warning boxes, and added more helpful messages when importing or saving fails.
- Moved the loading to the EditorThemeManager. If the theme loading fails (eg. if you delete while EditorSettings is open) then there will now be an error.
- Loaded themes now set the initial values, so they won't have revert buttons and now act more like the built in themes do.
- Project override for the `color_theme` now works. Note that if you do override it if you want to change any other colors, you will need to manually set the override to `Custom` or override the colors, as the EditorSettings values for them will be reverted.
- Moved the ScriptEditor ThemeMenu enum into its MenuOptions enum since values from both are used in the same switch statement and removing `THEME_SAVE` made the `THEME_SAVE_AS` option have the same value as another.
- Moved setting definitions out of GDScript highlighter.

Documentation
- Added documentation for the GDScript only syntax highlighter editor settings. I don't think we can only have them for GDScript builds, so its just always available now. The comment markers part could be used in other languages, but it isn't yet.
- In the GDScript syntax highlighter color descriptions, I use `[codeblock]` instead of `[code]`. This is because it has syntax highlighting in the editor, and it is useful to see the effects right there.

summary: Jolt Physics - Contact not reported from sleeping RigidBody3D when receiving collision

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.5.beta3.official [4d1f26e1f], v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta3 - Windows 11 (build 22621) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 Ti Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-12900H (20 threads) - 31.69 GiB memory

### Issue description

In _Jolt Physics_, a sleeping `RigidBody3D` will not wake when another moving `RigidBody3D` is nearby. This causes the first contact to be non-existent. In _GodotPhysics3D_, the sleeping `RigidBody3D` will wake when another moving `RigidBody3D` is nearby (I'm assuming, I don't know if it's distance based), and thus the first contact will be reported. Note that the collision behavior still occurs in that the objects move, but the first contact in _Jolt Physics_ isn't reported.

For clarification, "first contact" in _Jolt Physics_ refers to the contact information that is lost due to the hit `RigidBody3D` being asleep. The MRP is set up such that only one contact will occur.

I would expect the collision to always report a contact, regardless of the _Physics Engine_ set. As for the waking up of the `RigidBody3D`, I wouldn't necessarily expect it to wake up based on proximity, unless that is required for my expected behavior.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run Project
2. Ensure _Jolt Physics_ is set for _Physics Engine_
3. Hold "w" or "up arrow"
4. Wait until `Cube` and `Pusher` collide
5. Note how `There was a contact!` is never printed to the console
6. Ensure _GodotPhysics3D_ is set for _Physics Engine_
7. Hold "w" or "up arrow"
8. Wait until `Cube` and `Pusher` collide
9. Note how `There was a contact!` is now printed to the console
10. Also note how the cube woke up due to `Pusher` moving

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[jolt_sleep_mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21151896/jolt_sleep_mrp.zip)

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible on v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6] (Mac Os/Windows)



### System information

Mac OS Sequoia 15.5 - Windows 10 - Windows 11

### Issue description

As title says a missing filesystem_cache10 from .godot/editor triggers reimport for *all* assets.
I'm currently working on a project with around 15GBs of assets I'm committing on a git repo the .godot/imported directory to prevent the slow reimport process on every machine.
However, the reimport triggers anyways for all assets.

Reimporting is also very problematic as there seems to be a memory leak during import process, making the machines run out of memory and making us unable to re-run the import process on every machine.

Copying over filesystem_cache10 mitigates the issues if the files modification date is lower than what reported in filesystem_cache10 as godot does not test the assets for reimport

Committing filesystem_cache10 is obviously not a solution as it gets constantly updated by the different machines.

The problem dosen't seem related to the cache itself as when it's missing it just triggers a re-analysis of the files, it should be instead located in this function as it seems to detect changes even when there aren't. However I couldn't find any obvious mistake
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/4d1f26e1fd1fa46f2223fe0b6ac300744bf79b88/editor/file_system/editor_file_system.cpp#L573

I also don't have a compilation environment currently setup to test changes

### Steps to reproduce

- Create new project
- Add some assets and wait for import
- Close the project
- Delete .godot/engine/filesystem_cache10
- Open the project

All the assets will reimport

Previously, it was possible to set colour inside android/build/res/values/themes.xml like this ``` ``` In 4.5.beta2 this file is auto-generated, which is actually great, but the issue is, `windowSplashScreenBackground` is a reserved attribute, and even when I specify it in custom theme attributes during project export, it is ignored, based on this logic: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/4d1f26e1fd1fa46f2223fe0b6ac300744bf79b88/platform/android/export/export_plugin.cpp#L1089 I understand that beta version is not stable, but I need to use it because it's the only one that supports Android API 35, without which my app will be deleted from the store by the end of August, and this new splash background behaviour seems like a bug. Is there a different way to override the splash screen colour that I am missing? ### Steps to reproduce - Create a new project in Godot 4.5.beta2 - Install Android build template for 4.5.beta2 - set a custom theme attribute with key "[splash]android:windowSplashScreenBackground" and value "#031926" (any colour) - export project - value in themes.xml is unchanged, and when the app launches, the splash screen background is white instead of the provided colour ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: Severe performance regression (4.5.dev4 to 4.5.dev5) when showing large numbers of collision shapes in editor (or in-game if Visible Collision Shapes is enabled)

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in 4.5.dev5 and later (up to 4.5.beta3) - Non reproducible in 4.5.dev4 or previous versions, also not reproducible in 4.4.1 ### System information Windows 10 pro 22H2 - Godot 4.5.dev5 - Forward+ - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT ### Issue description This issue is very similar to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/105248 but please notice that that issue was about a performance regression from 4.3 to 4.4.1 (and that relates to the changes made in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/90644) and this issue is another big performance regression on top of that other one that starts with 4.5.dev5, and it might be caused by something totally different. The performance hit increases (sort of exponentially I think) with the number of collision shapes to show. The performance hit affects CPU TIME, not the FPS or the GPU in any way. So for example for 15000 collision shapes in 4.5.dev4: and the same 15000 collision shapes in 4.5.dev5: but now if we try with 49000 collision shapes, then the performance difference is much more noticeable, here is 49000 collision shapes in 4.5.dev4: and the same 49000 in 4.5.dev5: You can try those same scenes in the MRP I attach. It might also be helpful to mention that this was originally noticed in the game I am working on, which i cannot share here, but there I moved from being able to manage those scenes in editor without issues (even though 4.3 was giving a better performance than 4.4.1), like this scene with a CPU time of around 40 ms in 4.4.1 or 4.5.dev4: to 641 ms in 4.5.dev5 or 4.5.beta3: and I should also mention that sometimes, when opening that same scene with 4.5.dev5, i was getting non stop errors, like in the image below and interestingly, when that happened the performance was actually better, but maybe just because it wasn't rendering the whole thing, no idea. But then opening again the exact same project with the same scene might not give any errors: @mihe I hope it is ok to mention you here, since this is related to that other issue you were working on. ### Steps to reproduce Open the MRP with both 4.5.dev4 and 4.5.dev5 and compare results. You should check the scenes "scene3A_premade_scene_with_15000_meshes_and_collisions.tscn" and "scene3B_premade_scene_with_49000_meshes_and_collisions.tscn" Enable "View Frame Time" in the 3D editor to see the CPU Time and compare. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [collisions MRP 4.5.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21144091/collisions.MRP.4.5.zip)

summary: Editor and game crash with "Object was deleted while awaiting a callback." using a RichTextLabel and TextureRect in a HBoxContainer.

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.3-stable, 4.4.1-stable, 4.5-beta2. - Not reproducible in: 4.0.4-stable, 4.1.4-stable, 4.2.2-stable. (Although the behaviour is weird in those versions too.) Reproducible on Linux (Ubuntu 24.04 X11) and Windows 11. ### System information Godot v4.5.beta2 - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (nvidia; 535.230.02) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-4570 CPU @ 3.20GHz (4 threads) - 7.69 GiB memory ### Issue description A certain configuration of Control nodes causes Godot (both the running game, and the editor) to spew out a huge stream of "Object was deleted while awaiting a callback." messages, then segfault. This doesn't require any code. I was able to make a very small minimal reproduction which involves these three nodes: - A `HBoxContainer` with a set minimum size, and two children: - A `RichTextLabel` with `fit_content = true` and `scroll_active` = false, containing at least two words, but not enough words to fill up the minimum size of the container. - A `TextureRect` with an image loaded, `expand_mode = EXPAND_FIT_WIDTH`. Changing any of the properties mentioned above breaks the repro (e.g. having just one word in the label). So it seems to be something to do with the attempt to wrap words, while the texture rect is also doing some kind of dynamic sizing. I have seen various error messages on the console involving `Container::_sort_children` and `Control::_update_minimum_size` (inconsistently) which gives a clue. The expected behaviour is this: Earlier in my investigations, it seemed to require a text string of _exactly_ the available width, no more no less, to reproduce. But since I have minimized it, it now reproduces with any string smaller than the width. I am not sure what causes this change. On Linux, this spews out literally thousands of lines of "Object was deleted while awaiting a callback." and then after about 30 seconds, prints this: ``` TOTAL PAGES: 8192 (33554432 bytes). NULL count: 1392635. CALL null::null: 0. CALL null::null: 1. CALL null::null: 0. CALL null::null: 1. CALL null::null: 0. CALL null::null: 1. CALL Control::_update_minimum_size: 1. CALL null::null: 0. CALL null::null: 1. ``` And then the window loads with this obviously incorrect sizing: Throughout most of my investigation it was segfaulting and not loading any window, but in the most minimal example, I get the above weird behaviour instead. On Windows, the output is this: ``` Failed method: Container::_sort_children. Message queue out of memory. Message queue out of memory. Try increasing 'memory/limits/message_queue/max_size_mb' in project settings. Object was deleted while awaiting a callback. [repeats just 20 times] Segmentation fault ``` Note that opening this scene in the editor also (after about 30 seconds of freezing) crashes the entire editor with a segfault. However, opening the scene with one of the elements (e.g. the TextureRect) hidden, _then_ showing it, behaves as expected. Same with showing the texture at runtime after the scene has already loaded (e.g. in the editor, hide the texture, then run the program, then show the texture in the editor while the program is running). Prior to 4.3, it runs without crashing or freezing, but the texture is very large and the label is invisible. There are multiple other reports of this same error message (#104526, #97312) but they don't use the same setup and have gigantic repro projects, so I figured I would open a new bug with this very tiny repro project to investigate. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a Control hierarchy as described above. Other, more complex hierarchies work too, but this is the minimal set I could find that exhibits this behaviour. 2. Either run the project (to crash the game), or close and re-open the scene (to crash the editor). ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [rich-text-crash-repro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21122246/rich-text-crash-repro.zip) **Note**: This project crashes the Godot editor upon loading. To load it up, edit the file repro.tscn, adding "`visible = false`" to the TextureRect node. Then you can open it, manually make the TextureRect visible again, then see the crash.

summary: Texture2D importing doesn't expose compress/normal_map even though it still uses it.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.4.1.stable (49a5bc7b6) 

### System information

Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - AMD Ryzen 7 5700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

I was actually pulling my hair out on this one, apparently at some point I've changed my textures to Lossy and set normal map mode to Enabled and saved it as default preset for Texture2D.
After a while, everytime I was generating mipmaps for textures, the colors were getting extremely washed out, it was after a while of testing and narrowing down that I found out my texture.png.import had `compress/normal_map=1`, which isn't available for changing once you set compression mode to Lossless. (and exactly why I took forever to notice that was my problem all along).

I have no idea why that's hidden for lossless and available on all other options, but fixing it would be as simple as removing this IF in here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/4d1f26e1fd1fa46f2223fe0b6ac300744bf79b88/editor/import/resource_importer_texture.cpp#L203

### Steps to reproduce

- Open the texture import dock
- Set mode to Lossy
- set normal map to Enable
- Make sure generate mipmap is disabled
- Click Reimport

- Open the texture import dock again
- Set mode to Lossless
- Mark generate mipmaps and hit reimport

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[normalmapflag.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21122109/normalmapflag.zip)

summary: Text editor caret word skip is asymmetrical depending on direction

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.3, 4.4, 4.5

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700F CPU @ 2.90GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

When you use `Ctrl-Left` / `Ctrl-Right` or `Ctrl-Shift-Left` / `Ctrl-Shift-Right` in a text editor, the word skip is asymmetrical in how it behaviours going left vs. going right.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e5eedcea-c014-4733-9b64-81dd69cc9f1c)

It makes editing very annoying, because I can't get to the word boundary I want without doing some weird logical mental gymnastics and going back and forth a few times with and without the `Ctrl` key being held down (like parallel parking for text).

I'm not sure if it's intentional, but I have never seen any other text editor behave this way. I've seen various ways of determining *where the word boundaries are*, but at least moving between them is always a *symmetrical* operation.

### Steps to reproduce

Open any script, and use `Ctrl-Left` / `Ctrl-Right` on any line of more than a few "words". Notice that whenever you go left, the caret lands on the left side of the words, but whenever you go right, the caret lands on the right side of the words, meaning you never land on the same place when you go too far and try to go back.
